Title: A quantitative trait locus, qSE3, promotes seed germination and seedling establishment under salinity stress in rice

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: KT/HAK/KUP family K+ transporter; HAK transporter; 12 transmembrane domains
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Primarily expressed in root, followed by stem; transcript increases in filling grain from 21 DAF and sharply at mature stage; strongly induced in germinating seeds by salinity stress after 36 h imbibition
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Primarily expressed in root; induced by salinity stress during germination
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: mineral nutrient; hormone; reactive oxygen species
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: K+ homeostasis and salinity adaptation; ABA mediates stress signaling; reduced H2O2 lowers oxidative damage and supports germination/seedling establishment under salinity
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes seed germination and seedling establishment under salinity stress by increasing K+ uptake and also increasing Na+ uptake in germinating seeds, elevating ABA accumulation/signaling, and reducing H2O2 level
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: QTL mapping; map-based cloning; near-isogenic line analysis; T-DNA mutant (oshak21) analysis; qRT-PCR; promoter-GUS expression assay; yeast complementation in K+ uptake-deficient CY162; ICP-MS ion measurement; ABA quantification by HPLC; H2O2 and antioxidant enzyme assays; haplotype association analysis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. qSE3 was fine-mapped and cloned as OsHAK21, a K+ transporter underlying a major QTL for seed germination and seedling establishment under salinity stress. 2. The Jiucaiqing allele showed higher expression under salinity stress and stronger K+ transport activity than the IR26 allele. 3. qSE3 increased K+ and Na+ uptake in germinating seeds under salinity stress without significantly lowering the Na+/K+ ratio. 4. qSE3 elevated ABA level, upregulated ABA signaling-associated genes, decreased H2O2 accumulation, and increased CAT, APX and POD activities. 5. HAP3 and SNP 2036T of qSE3 were positively associated with improved germination under salinity stress. 6. qSE3 improved direct-seeding performance under salinity stress without obvious agronomic penalties reported.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Salinity stress during germination and direct seeding (200 mM or 300 mM NaCl depending on background); also evaluated under PEG and low-temperature stress
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: SNP haplotype analysis using rice accession SNP dataset from Rice Diversity database (McCouch et al., 2016); no accession number reported
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: OsHAK21 native promoter fused to GUS; inducible by salinity stress
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious penalties in measured agronomic traits including heading date, plant height, effective tillers, 1000-grain weight and panicle length in NIL(qSE3) versus IR26; however qSE3 also increases Na+ uptake in germinating seeds
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for breeding rice adapted to direct seeding under saline conditions; favorable HAP3/2036T allele can be selected for marker-assisted breeding; promising because improvement occurred without obvious agronomic penalties
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper classifies qSE3 as a K+ transporter but notes the mechanism for increased Na+ uptake remains unresolved and may be indirect via activation of other ion channels; elite allele from Jiucaiqing is similar in ORF to Nipponbare and differs from IR26 by six coding SNPs and promoter polymorphisms; strongest GUS signal observed in root; oshak21 mutant shows reduced germination/seedling establishment under salinity but not under normal conditions
